message:
fix(mcp): enforce workspace containment for mission paths (CRITICAL, contains MCP read/write to workspace root)

ft-security-mcp-path-traversal

MCP tools `wa.tx_plan`, `wa.tx_show`, `wa.tx_run`, `wa.tx_rollback`,
`wa.mission_state`, `wa.mission_explain`, `wa.mission_pause`,
`wa.mission_resume`, and `wa.mission_abort` accept optional
`contract_file` / `mission_file` string arguments. Prior to this fix
the helpers `mcp_resolve_mission_tx_file_path` and
`mcp_resolve_mission_file_path` at mcp_missions.rs:28 and :136
converted the supplied path with `PathBuf::from(path)` and passed it
straight to `std::fs::read_to_string` (contract_path) or
`std::fs::write` (save_to_path). No workspace containment check, no
`..` filter, no canonicalization.

Impact: any MCP client (stdio, local IPC, or a malicious MCP proxy)
could supply `contract_file: "/etc/passwd"` and exfiltrate arbitrary
files under the watcher's UID, or supply a writable absolute path
and overwrite any file the watcher can touch. `..` traversal from
within the workspace was equally unchecked.

Fix (mcp_missions.rs):
- Introduce `resolve_workspace_scoped_path(workspace_root, user_path)`
  that (a) rejects any `..` component outright, (b) joins relative
  paths to the workspace root, leaves absolute paths alone, and
  (c) canonicalizes the nearest existing ancestor and asserts
  `starts_with(workspace_root.canonicalize()?)`. Save paths against
  not-yet-existing targets are supported via the nearest-ancestor
  walk.
- Route both `mcp_resolve_mission_tx_file_path` and
  `mcp_resolve_mission_file_path` through the helper before returning
  a PathBuf to callers.
- Error surfaces use MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS with distinct messages
  (`parent-directory component`, `escapes workspace root`) that are
  asserted by the test suite.

Tests (5 new + 2 updated):
- `resolve_workspace_scoped_path_rejects_parent_dir_component`:
  `../../../etc/passwd` rejected before any FS touch.
- `resolve_workspace_scoped_path_rejects_absolute_path_outside_root`:
  `/etc/passwd` rejected.
- `resolve_workspace_scoped_path_accepts_relative_under_root`:
  `mission/contract.json` under a tempdir workspace resolves.
- `resolve_workspace_scoped_path_accepts_save_path_for_nonexistent_target`:
  `mission/not-yet.json` is accepted via nearest-ancestor canonical.
- `resolve_workspace_scoped_path_accepts_absolute_path_inside_root`:
  absolute path under the workspace is accepted.
- `resolve_tx_file_path_explicit` + `resolve_mission_file_path_explicit`
  (pre-existing): updated to expect rejection for the prior attack
  vector `/tmp/my-tx.json` / `/tmp/my-mission.json`; assertion flips
  from `Ok(PathBuf::from(...))` to `Err(MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS /
  "escapes workspace root")`.

Compile-only verification to come via a narrow
`cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp_missions::tests::containment --no-run`
run; full-lib cargo test was hanging upstream under unrelated mux
crate build breakage. If the narrow compile surfaces any regression,
a follow-up commit will land before beyond this check-in.
